comparison_summary.table_extraction_accuracy: GPT-4.1-mini (both low and rotated) consistently extracted full donor details with correct names, amounts, and payment methods, preserving exact formatting and numbers. GPT-4.1-nano versions had minor inaccuracies in donor names (e.g., 'EcoMart' as 'EcoMat', 'Rahul Verma' shortened to 'Verma') and inconsistent formatting of amounts (presence/absence of $ sign, mixing strings and floats). The rotated GPT-4.1-nano lost some formatting consistency and had more noticeable errors in donor names.
comparison_summary.multi_column_layout_preservation: GPT-4.1-mini with low detail preserved the column headers and nested content (such as 'Our Mission' as title, bullet points properly grouped, full subheaders). The text was structured by sections with proper keys. GPT-4.1-nano produced flat or incomplete column arrays, treating all items as a simple list of strings rather than structured sections, losing hierarchical grouping. The rotated GPT-4.1-nano simplified columns into few concatenated paragraphs, losing detailed segmentation. GPT-4.1-mini (rotated) maintained better structured multi-column layout despite rotation.
comparison_summary.handwriting_transcription_quality: GPT-4.1-mini detail low had very high accuracy and preserved phrasing exactly, including emojis and special characters, with minimal/no corrections. GPT-4.1-nano cleaned text more aggressively, fixing spacing (removing extra spaces or adding punctuation), normalizing some case or formatting (adding a space in 'Next Month:' or changing '@' to 'at'), which somewhat altered the original handwriting style but improved clarity. Both handled handwritten lists well. GPT-4.1-mini rotated had similar high quality transcription to mini low with almost verbatim accuracy. GPT-4.1-nano rotated contained fewer lines and was partial, missing several handwritten notes.
comparison_summary.robustness_to_image_rotation: GPT-4.1-mini showed strong robustness to rotated input, maintaining table completeness, column structure, and handwriting accuracy with slight formatting tweaks but no data loss. GPT-4.1-nano showed degradation when rotated: more errors in table donor names, incomplete handwriting extraction, and loss of structure in columns, indicating less robustness to orientation changes.

detail_low_losses.lost_information: Using detail low primarily caused minimal losses. The main loss was some minor text normalization in handwriting (e.g., removed star emoji from 'Plastic-free campaign' and kept checklist unicode). Otherwise, all table data and complex layout elements were fully preserved, including bullet points and detailed section titles.

gpt41mini_vs_nano.layout_understanding: GPT-4.1-mini demonstrated significantly stronger layout understanding than GPT-4.1-nano. Mini retained multi-level nested structures for columns (headers, titles, bullet points, contact as key-value), while nano output columns as flat arrays or concatenated paragraphs without meaningful structure. Mini captured tables accurately with proper column headers, types, and data as strings or floats, whereas nano had partial or inaccurate donor names and less consistent numerical formatting. Mini also preserved handwritten note formatting with exact text and special characters, while nano normalized or truncated handwritten content.

rotation_effects.gpt41mini: Rotation had minimal impact. The mini model produced nearly identical table extractions and multilevel column layouts, and handwritten notes were transcribed with the same high fidelity and very minor formatting differences.
rotation_effects.gpt41nano: Rotation negatively affected nano more: donor names were more incomplete or slightly corrupted (e.g., 'EcoMart' to 'EcoMat'), handwritten notes were shortened and partially missed, and column text was simplified, losing multi-column dividing cues. Overall, rotated nano outputs were poorer quality and less complete.

best_overall_setting.reasoning: Both provide the most accurate and complete tabular data extraction with correct formatting, robust multi-column semantic layout preservation, and near-verbatim handwriting transcription, showing robustness even under rotation. They have consistent output structure facilitating easier downstream use. Among these, the original orientation (gpt41mini_low_original) has a slightly more comprehensive columns field with explicit keys and preserved bullet points, making it overall the best.
